茜.name: Madder
茜.meaning: Madder; also the reddish color or dye made from madder roots.
茜.reviewNote: Checked akane, sen, and sei readings, madder, madder dye, and madder red glosses, and 艹 + 西 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芋.name: Tuber
芋.meaning: Edible tuber such as potato, sweet potato, yam, or taro.
芋.reviewNote: Checked imo and u readings, edible tuber glosses, and 艹 + 于 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

英.name: English
英.reviewNote: Checked ei and hanabusa readings, Britain, English, and outstanding glosses, and 艹 + 央 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

花.name: Flower
花.meaning: Flower; a basic kanji for blossoms and flowers.
花.reviewNote: Checked hana and ka readings, flower gloss, and 艹 + 化 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苛.name: Harsh
苛.meaning: Harsh or severe; also to bully or torment.
苛.reviewNote: Checked kibishii, ka, and ga readings, harsh, severe, bully, and torment glosses, and 艹 + 可 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茄.name: Eggplant
茄.reviewNote: Checked nasu and ka readings, eggplant gloss, and 艹 + 加 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

荷.name: Load

荷.reviewNote: Checked ni, ninau, and ka readings, load, cargo, and luggage glosses, and 艹 + 何 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菓.name: Confectionery

菓.reviewNote: Checked kashi, kudamono, and ka readings, confection or fruit usage, and 艹 + 果 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芹.name: Water Dropwort
芹.meaning: Water dropwort, an edible plant also counted among the seven spring herbs.
芹.reviewNote: Checked seri, kin, and gon readings, water dropwort gloss, seven spring herbs note, and 艹 + 斤 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芽.name: Bud
芽.meaning: Bud, sprout, or shoot; also the germ or beginning of an idea.
芽.reviewNote: Checked me and ga readings, bud, sprout, shoot, and germ glosses, and 艹 + 牙 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芥.name: Mustard
芥.meaning: Mustard; also rubbish, trash, or dust.
芥.reviewNote: Checked karashi, akuta, kai, and ke readings, mustard, rubbish, trash, and dust glosses, and 艹 + 介 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苦.name: Bitter
苦.meaning: Bitter; also pain, suffering, or hardship.
苦.reviewNote: Checked nigai, kurushii, and ku readings, bitter and suffering glosses, and 艹 + 古 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

若.name: Young
若.meaning: Young; also used for conditional meanings such as if.
若.reviewNote: Checked wakai and jaku readings, young and if glosses, and 艹 + 右 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芯.name: Core
芯.meaning: Core, heart, wick, or pencil lead.
芯.reviewNote: Checked shin, hosoi, and toshingusa readings, core, heart, wick, and pencil lead glosses, and 艹 + 心 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

草.name: Grass

草.reviewNote: Checked kusa and so readings, grass, weed, and herb glosses, and 艹 + 早 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苗.name: Seedling

苔.name: Moss

芳.name: Fragrant

芳.reviewNote: Checked kanbashii and ho readings, fragrant, good reputation, and satisfactory glosses, and 艹 + 方 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.
